data.title: Pfizer RSV vaccine offered to 3M more older adults in England
data.description:
England’s National Health Service announced on Thursday that three million more older adults in the country will be eligible to receive Pfizer’s (PFE) respiratory syncytial virus ((RSV)) vaccine, Abrysvo.

Accordingly, people aged 80 years and older, as well as those living in a care home for older adults, will be able to receive Abrysvo via a GP practice, community pharmacies, or by informing employees in care homes.

The decision, taking effect on Apr. 2, is expected to prevent potentially thousands of hospital admissions caused by the virus, which causes bronchitis and pneumonia, among other symptoms, NHS England said. The RSV accounts for nearly 9,000 hospitalizations in people older than 75 in the U.K. each year.

Previously, the NHS offered the bivalent shot as part of its RSV vaccination programs for adults aged 75-79 years old since the program launched in Sept. 2024. Pregnant women also received Abrysvo from 28 weeks of their pregnancy to protect newborns from RSV.

data.title: Oil soars to $110/bbl as Trump threatens war escalation; Macron says unrealistic to take Hormuz
data.description:
U.S. crude oil futures skyrocketed past $110/bbl Thursday after President ‌Trump rattled markets with his promise to escalate attacks on Iran over the next 2-3 weeks, sparking fears of prolonged disruptions to oil supply.

Meanwhile, French President Macron ‌said it would be "unrealistic" to launch a military operation to force open the Strait of Hormuz, after Trump challenged countries most impacted by the shutoff to work towards reopening it.

U.S. front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery surged 11.3% to $111.49/bbl, the highest in four years, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery soared 7% to $108.28/bbl, still below highs near $120 touched earlier in the conflict.

Oil and gas equities, which fell sharply in the previous session, turned broadly higher in Thursday's trading - although below highs posted at the open - led by Diamondback Energy (FANG) up 3%, Texas Pacific Land (TPL) up 2%, ConocoPhillips (COP) up 1.9%, Chevron (CVX) up 1.8%.

Markets are reacting to the absence of any "clear mention of ceasefire or diplomatic engagement" in the speech, Phillip Nova senior analyst Priyanka Sachdeva said in a note. "If tensions intensify or maritime risks increase, oil could test fresh ​highs as markets price in potential supply disruptions."

OPEC+ reportedly will consider a further oil output increase when eight members meet on ‌Sunday, a move that would position key producers to add more barrels if the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)

data.title: U.S. Navy destroyer U.S.S. George M. Neal launched at Ingalls Shipbuilding
data.description:
The Ingalls Shipbuilding division of HII (HII) on Wednesday launched the future U.S.S. George M. Neal (DDG 131), marking a key milestone in construction of the U.S. Navy’s next generation of guided missile destroyers.

 

 

The vessel is the fourth Flight III Arleigh Burke-class destroyer to be built at the Mississippi shipyard. Prior to launch, shipbuilders completed structural work, installed major components and conducted inspections to prepare the ship for entry into the water.

The launch process involved moving the ship from land into a dry dock, where final checks were carried out before the dock was flooded, allowing the vessel to float for the first time.

The destroyer is named after George M. Neal, a Korean War aviation machinist’s mate who received the Navy Cross for attempting to rescue a fellow service member under enemy fire.

DDG 131 is part of the Flight III configuration, which includes updated systems intended to address evolving threats. These ships are equipped with the AN/SPY-6 ((V))1 radar and the Aegis Baseline 10 combat system, both designed to enhance air and missile defense capabilities.

Following the launch, the ship will undergo outfitting, activation of onboard systems and testing before entering sea trials, a standard phase ahead of delivery to the U.S. Navy.

data.title: Chili's performance and new chicken sandwich earn an upgrade for Brinker International
data.description:
The strength of the Chili’s segment will continue to drive growth for Brinker International (EAT) with KeyBanc expecting this momentum to continue in FY27, driving wider margins and higher valuation for the stock.

This earned the company an upgrade to Overweight from Sector Weight and a $177 price target that represents 23% upside to Wednesday’s closing price.

“We believe underlying sales trends for Chili’s have remained strong in recent months, despite interruption by severe winter weather,” writes KeyBanc analyst Christopher Carril, modeling same restaurant sales for the chain at +6% for Q4 2026 (versus +5.8% consensus) and +5.6% in FY27 (versus +4.2% consensus).

Coupled with corresponding margin expansion, Carril anticipates a tailwind to multiple expansion as shares currently trade below recent historical and peer averages.

This upbeat outlook is amplified by key top-line drivers, including a new chicken sandwich platform launching at Chili’s in April, new menu upgrades and innovations, continued marketing support, and the “ongoing benefit from a service model that has seen substantial investment and improvement in recent years.”

Carril also raised his estimates for fiscal 2026 EPS to $10.72 from $10.65 (consensus $10.71) and revenue estimates to $5.82B from $5.81B ($5.81B consensus estimates).

Brinker International (EAT) will report fiscal Q3 results before the open on April 29, expected to have earned an adjusted profit of $2.88 per share on $1.48B in revenue.

data.title: Postal Realty tops small-cap ROE growth list, as market prepares to ride out volatility
data.description:
Higher rates are a growing headwind for stocks: the higher they go, the more pressure they put on equities. After a month of conflict, strategists are likely to start lowering year-end targets as oil prices keep inflation fears alive and the market stays focused on just one big issue.

For now, the best approach is to ride out the volatility by owning stocks with strong company-specific strength, experts suggest. From a quant view, that means names with high return on equity and solid EPS growth.

Below is a list of the top 10 small-cap stocks across various sectors, including REITs, education, and financials, ranked by their year-over-year return on equity growth.

The list is topped by Postal Realty Trust, Inc. (PSTL), with a year-over-year ROE growth of 98.89%. American Public Education, Inc. (APEI) and WisdomTree, Inc. (WT) follow closely behind, with Willdan Group, Inc. (WLDN) and Southern First Bancshares, Inc. (SFST) rounding out the top five.

The list also features notable performers from the utilities, technology, and automotive sectors. Suburban Propane Partners, L.P. (SPH), EverQuote, Inc. (EVER), and Red Violet, Inc. (RDVT) all demonstrate strong ROE growth, while many of the companies on this list maintain excellent EPS revision grades, with several earning A+ ratings.

Here is the list:

  1. Postal Realty Trust, Inc. (PSTL), YOY ROE growth: 98.89%, EPS revision: A+

  2. American Public Education, Inc. (APEI), YOY ROE growth: 94.93%, EPS revision: A-

  3. WisdomTree, Inc. (WT), YOY ROE growth: 89.38%, EPS revision: A

  4. Willdan Group, Inc. (WLDN), YOY ROE growth: 87.51%, EPS revision: A+

  5. Southern First Bancshares, Inc. (SFST), YOY ROE growth: 79.82%, EPS revision: A

  6. Suburban Propane Partners, L.P. (SPH), YOY ROE growth: 79.73%, EPS revision: A-

  7. EverQuote, Inc. (EVER), YOY ROE growth: 78.81%, EPS revision: A+

  8. Red Violet, Inc. (RDVT), YOY ROE growth: 73.00%, EPS revision: A+

  9. ePlus inc. (PLUS), YOY ROE growth: 67.05%, EPS revision: A+

  10. Strattec Security Corporation (STRT), YOY ROE growth: 55.28%, EPS revision: A

Small-Cap ETFs: (IJR), (IWM), (VB), (VBR), (VBK), (SCHA), (SPSM), and (DFAS)

data.title: The best hedge against oil-based inflation is oil itself – strategist
data.description:
The best way for investors to protect themselves from oil-driven inflation shocks is to hold oil itself, according to Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi, UBS Global Wealth Management’s chief investment officer for the Americas and global head of equities.

In an interview with CNBC, Hoffmann-Burchardi said her firm remains constructive on equities but emphasized that diversification across sectors and asset classes has become crucial for navigating the current geopolitical landscape.

UBS significantly shifted its investment approach at the end of last year, moving away from a concentrated bet on technology (VGT), (XLK), (IYW) that had defined its strategy for over two years.

“We need to broaden out. We need to diversify across the different sectors of the economy,” Hoffmann-Burchardi said, noting this diversification is even more important now given the ongoing conflict involving Iran.

While acknowledging significant uncertainty around geopolitics, Hoffmann-Burchardi pointed to several constraints that may limit the duration of the conflict. She cited the upcoming U.S. midterm elections in November, noting that “prolonged military conflicts have not helped presidential popularity ratings,” referencing the historical examples of Presidents Truman and Johnson.

Iran’s weakened economy and military heading into the conflict, combined with international pressure over disrupted supply chains for fertilizer inputs and helium, suggest “the probability of a resolution increases, even if we might see more short-term escalation.”

On the technology front, Hoffmann-Burchardi said artificial intelligence (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC) remains underappreciated in terms of both capabilities and disruption risks.

“AI used to be just a digital coworker. Now it’s autonomously writing code, and not only that, but it’s also self-checking the quality of its code,” she explained, describing the growth in coding capabilities over the past three to four months as “super exponential.”

Despite her bullishness on AI’s potential, UBS has downgraded the technology and communication services (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC) sectors due to disruption risks across the digital economy.

Hoffmann-Burchardi raised questions about how agentic AI will affect advertising, shopping, and consumer behavior, noting, “There's disruption risk across the broader part of the digital economy.”

She also pointed to upcoming IPOs that could encroach on the competitive advantages of existing major technology companies.

data.title: Akamai downgraded to Neutral at Baird on 'more balanced' risk-reward
data.description:
• Akamai Technologies (AKAM) was downgraded to Neutral from Outperform at Baird on Thursday, as the investment firm said the risk-reward in the stock has become “more balanced.”
  • “AKAM has outperformed significantly YTD (+33% vs. S&P 500 -4%, IGV -25%), rallying 71% above 52-week lows (recently made 52-week highs), driven by optimism around compute upside and AI/agentic traffic tailwinds,” analyst William Power wrote in a note to clients. “While those could provide long-term tailwinds, we don't expect significant near- term agentic uplift. In addition, seasonal factors could limit Q1 upside (in constant-currency), particularly for its key CIS segment. While positive on the long-term CIS opportunity, we now view risk/reward as more balanced at 17.4x EPS and 23x FCF.”
  • Looking deeper, Power, who has a $110 price target on Akamai, added that any benefit from agentic artificial intelligence is likely to be “modest,” at best. “Simply put, we are skeptical that text-based AI activity most prevalent today is traffic-intensive enough to significantly move the needle on AKAM's Delivery business,” Power added. “We think tailwinds could be more meaningful longer-term, as the proliferation of agents takes hold, or audio/video-based AI activity becomes more prevalent.”

data.title: Anthropic says no sensitive data exposed in leak of code behind Claude AI agent
data.description:
Anthropic (ANTHRO) said no sensitive customer data or credentials were exposed after accidentally revealing the underlying instructions it uses to direct its AI agent app Claude Code.

"Earlier today, a Claude Code release included some internal source code. No sensitive customer data or credentials were involved or exposed. This was a release packaging issue caused by human error, not a security breach. We're rolling out measures to prevent this from happening again," said an Anthropic spokesperson in an email to Seeking Alpha.

By Wednesday morning, Anthropic representatives had used a copyright takedown request to remove over 8,000 copies and adaptations of the raw Claude Code instructions — known as source code — that developers had shared on programming platform GitHub, the Wall Street Journal reported.

The AI tech giant later narrowed its takedown request to cover only 96 copies and adaptations, saying its initial ask had reached more GitHub accounts than intended, the report noted.

The leak did reveal commercially sensitive information, including Anthropic's proprietary techniques, tools, and instructions for enabling its AI models to work as coding agents. Those techniques and tools are called a harness because they are what allow users to control and direct those models, the report noted.

The result is that Anthropic’s rivals and several startups and developers now have a road map to clone Claude Code’s features without needing to reverse engineer them, something that is already common, the report added.

The Claude Code information was accidentally disclosed on Tuesday when the company updated the AI tool. Like most proprietary software, Claude’s source code is usually difficult to reverse engineer. However, this time, the company posted to GitHub a type of file that linked back to the source code that outsiders could download and interpret, as per the report.

An X user noticed the leak and spread the word. Within hours, copies were multiplying.

Programmers going through the source code have been impressed at some of Anthropic’s tricks for getting Claude AI models to operate as Claude Code. One feature requests the models to go back periodically through tasks and consolidate their memories — a process it calls dreaming. Another seems to instruct Claude Code in some cases to go “undercover” and not reveal that it is an AI when publishing code to platforms like GitHub, the report noted.

After Anthropic asked that GitHub remove copies of its proprietary code, another programmer used other AI tools to rewrite the Claude Code functionality in other programming languages. Writing on GitHub, the programmer said the effort was intended to keep the information available without risking a takedown. That new version has become popular on the programming platform, the report added.

The leak is useful because it names hidden features and upcoming models, but it is unlikely to be of use to hackers, the report noted, citing Dan Guido, chief executive of cybersecurity firm Trail of Bits. 

Guido added that hackers already could reverse engineer the code before the leak, while Claude Code is frequently rewritten, meaning the leak will soon become obsolete, the report noted.

Last week, it was reported that Anthropic's latest AI model, Claude Mythos, which is also its most powerful, was revealed through a data leak.

Reports of the new model went public the same night that Anthropic — which is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google — won a court order blocking a Trump Administration ban on government use of its AI models.

Anthropic, reportedly, was seeking a stay from the ​U.S. appeals court on a Pentagon order designating it as a supply-chain risk, pending a judicial review of the case. The company sued the U.S. Department of War after the department dropped its contract with the AI startup and labeled it a supply-chain risk. Anthropic said in court filings that the U.S. government’s action of blacklisting the company could reduce the company's 2026 revenue by multiple billions of dollars.

Anthropic PBC is also reportedly weighing an IPO as soon as October, as it competes with rival OpenAI (OPENAI) in the IPO race.

Update: The story was updated with a statement from Anthropic.

data.title: Wall Street falls as investors digest Trump’s address to the nation
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages pushed lower on Thursday as market sentiment soured following President Donald Trump’s address to the nation, signaling a potential escalation in military action against Iran.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last at -1.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was 1%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, seven of the 11 S&P segments traded in the red, with consumer discretionary at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, energy was the strongest performer so far as oil (CL1:COM) spiked over 10%. 

Trump said the U.S. will strike Iran “extremely hard” over the next two to three weeks, warning the country would be pushed “back to the stone ages,” though he indicated talks with Tehran remain ongoing. 

Treasury yields were mostly muted across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) ticked up 1 basis point to 4.33%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) inched up 1 basis point to 3.81%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) added 1 basis point to 4.91%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims  for the week ended March 28 decreased by 9K to 202K, compared to the prior week's revised level of 211K and against the 213K consensus figure.

As for stocks that were on the move, APA Corporation (APA) climbed +2.9%, while shares of United Airlines (UAL) dropped -5.5%.
